params.name: Lyot Coronagraph
params.description:
On Mar 12, 1930 AD, Bernard Lyot invented the first coronagraph.
A coronagraph is a specialized telescope that enables astronomers to photograph the Sun's corona without waiting for a solar eclipse to occur. When comparing photographs of the Sun's corona during each solar eclipse, astronomers found that the corona's shape varies each time. To study how the corona changes with the phenomena of the Sun, long-term observation of the corona is necessary. However, a person's lifetime will only exp …
